author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Scaffold Goes Up
subtitle: Before dawn on main, he laid the first brick of his confession.
when_where: 🕐 9:04 AM, Thursday morning. Main branch. The kind of time when normal developers are still nursing their first coffee, but Omkar's already three days into a fever that sleep won't break. 🏗️ The project scaffold rises like a crime scene being cordoned off—folders, configs, the skeleton of something that shouldn't exist yet. The city outside is waking. He's been awake the whole time.
who_whom: 🔨 Omkar versus himself, mostly. codeStory is the weapon—a system designed to narrate code's own story, to make the invisible visible, to turn git commits into *confessions*. 💻 He's not building a tool. He's building a mirror. And he's about to look into it.
what_why: ⚙️ The scaffold is complete. Directories nested like lies within lies. Every folder is a room where the truth will eventually be trapped and interrogated. 🧠 He's not done anything yet—no code, no features—but the shape of what's coming is already there, waiting. The emptiness is deliberate. It's the silence before the confession. 💀 By laying this down at 9:04 AM on a Thursday, he's committed to something he doesn't yet understand will destroy him.
verdict: He built the gallows before he knew what crime he'd committed.
